settings.title: Settings

videos.label: Settings - Adding SDC
videos.description: An SDC can be added to the test if one has already been created.

videos.label: Settings - Sampling Rate
videos.description: Sets the data sampling rate for the test from 1Hz to 1000Hz. Default is 50Hz (Hz is a data sample per second. Data is sampled for each measurement axis (Load, Displacement, Time, Stress and Strain (if selected).

videos.label: Settings - SI Units
videos.description: The SI Units will be used to set the global parameters for each operation.

videos.label: Settings - Load Protection
videos.description: Defines a Tension and Compression limit that can be used to protect fixtures and grips that are used when they are rated below the capacity of the loadcell used.

videos.label: Settings - Break Threshold
videos.description: A number entered is a percentage of the value of the size of the loadcell that is used with the test. Below this value the break detector is not ON, above this value it is active.

videos.label: Settings - Break Drop
videos.description: A number entered is a percentage of a fall in load from the maximum load detected during the test. If the fall in force exceeds this percentage the sample is deemed to have broken.

videos.label: Settings - Sharp Break
videos.description: Changes the method of break detection to a fall in force that happens very quickly as in a sharp fall in force in a short period of time.

videos.label: Settings - Test Automation
videos.description: Will automatically repeat the test for a defined number of samples with a user defined time delay between each test. It can also stop the automation if a sample fails.

videos.label: Settings - Dynamic
videos.description: Enabling this feature by turning it on and choosing >= or <= and enter a force value in the Delta box. When the load is above or below this value data sampling rate is reduced.

videos.label: Settings - ELS Invert
videos.description: Enabling this feature will allow loadcells to be used upside down, where load reading is not affected by orientation and movement operations are inverted to reflect the direction of travel.

batch-export.title: Batch Export

videos.label: Batch Export - Batch Export - Text or CSV
videos.description: The batch export feature supports text or CSV formats and includes an automatic export option, which can be enabled to automate batch exports. Users can choose to overwrite the same export file with each test or export files per batch. Additionally, exports can be configured per batch set, with the option to add a prefix to file names. The file location and export file name can also be customized.

videos.label: Batch Export - Batch Export - Excel
videos.description: The Excel export feature allows users to customize file exports by adding a prefix to the file name, selecting the export location, and enabling automatic export. Automatic exports can be configured per batch or batch set, with an option to overwrite the same file for each test. Users can choose to display the Excel file after export and specify the sheet, column, and row for exporting results.

videos.description: The Excel export feature offers customization options, including adding a file name prefix, selecting the export location, and enabling automatic export at the end of each test. Additionally, the exported Excel file can be displayed automatically, and users can specify the sheet, column, and row for exporting results.

specimen-results.title: Specimen Results

videos.label: Specimen Results - Young's Modulus - Use by Other Results
videos.description: Young’s modulus or modulus of elasticity calculation. Is derived from an algorithm that determines the greatest linear slope within specified boundary conditions.

videos.label: Specimen Results - Young's Modulus - User Defined Points
videos.description: Young’s modulus or modulus of elasticity calculation. Is derived from an algorithm that determines the greatest linear slope within specified boundary conditions.

videos.label: Specimen Results - Offset Yield
videos.description: Offset % Additional Result Offset yield is calculated using the Stress vs Strain graph and provides the intercept between a line parallel to and offset from the Young's modulus value. The offset value in percentage can be entered here.

videos.label: Specimen Results - X-Intercept
videos.description: Value of Y Calculates the strain value at a specified stress value, the x value is determined using an offset gradient from the Young's modulus calculation. It is expressed as a percentage of the original gauge length.

videos.label: Specimen Results - ISO 6892 Break
videos.description: Additional Result Determines the break value using the algorithm from ISO 6892.

videos.label: Specimen Results - ISO 6892 Break X Intercept
videos.description: Calculates the strain value at the ISO 6892 calculated breakpoint, the x value is determined using an offset gradient from the Young's modulus calculation.

videos.label: Specimen Results - ISO 6982 Ag
videos.description: Calculates Ag the percentage strain value of the plastic extension recorded at the maximum force point in accordance with ISO 6892. The line is calculated at the same gradient as the Young's modulus calculation. It is expressed as a percentage of the original gauge length.

videos.label: Specimen Results - ISO 6982 Ae
videos.description: User defined Points Calculates Ae the percentage strain value yield point measured over the distance from the start of yielding to the start of the uniform work-hardening, in discontinuous materials. In accordance with ISO 6892. It is expressed as a percentage of the original gauge length.

standard-results.title: Standard Results

videos.label: Standard Results - Area
videos.description: The area under a curve. When load vs displacement, this represents energy or work done. With stress vs strain, this is equal to the toughness of the material or modulus of resilience (If calculation boundaries limit calculation to the elastic region).

videos.label: Standard Results - Best Fit
videos.description: Trendline: the gradient of a straight line of best fit through all the data points within an x-range, and the y-intercept of this line.

videos.label: Standard Results - Slope
videos.description: Trendline: the gradient of a straight line of best fit through the first and last data points within an x-range, and the y-intercept of this line. Available with User Defined Points

videos.label: Standard Results - Average
videos.description: Mean (sum of all data values divided by the number of point values).

videos.label: Standard Results - Break - End of Graph Defined
videos.description: The Break calculation returns the load, displacement, stress or strain within a defined range before the end of a test.

videos.label: Standard Results - Peak
videos.description: % Drop Occurrence Additional Result Peaks are defined by percentage drop after rising in y value. Select which peak in order of occurrence in time sequence.

videos.label: Standard Results - Number of Peaks
videos.description: % Drop The total number of peaks is defined by percentage drop and x/y range settings.

videos.label: Standard Results - Average of Peaks
videos.description: % Drop Additional Result The average value for all peaks within the defined boundaries. This is defined by percentage drop and x/y range settings.

videos.label: Standard Results - Trough
videos.description: % Drop Occurrence Additional Result Troughs are defined by percentage rise after falling in y value. Select which trough by time sequence.

videos.label: Standard Results - Number of Troughs
videos.description: % Drop The total number of troughs is defined by percentage rise and x/y range settings.

videos.label: Standard Results - Average of Troughs
videos.description: % Drop Additional Result The average value for all troughs is within the defined boundaries. This is defined by percentage drop and x/y range settings.

videos.label: Standard Results - Value
videos.description: Defined Point Occurrence Additional Result The value of y for a specified value of x or the value of x for a specified value of y.

videos.label: Standard Results - [Peak]
videos.description: % Drop Order Additional Result Peaks are defined by percentage drop after rising in y value. Select which peak by magnitude order.

videos.label: Standard Results - [Trough]
videos.description: % Drop Order Additional Result Troughs are defined by percentage rise after falling in y value. Select which trough by magnitude order.

videos.label: Standard Results - Max
videos.description: Additional Result Maximum y-axis value within a range on the x-axis.

videos.label: Standard Results - Min
videos.description: Additional Result Minimum y-axis value within a range on the x-axis.

videos.label: Standard Results - Percentage Break
videos.description: % Drop Additional Results Percentage break, as defined using drop percentage from a maximum.

videos.label: Standard Results - Percentage Break X Intercept
videos.description: % Drop X-Intercept value at the percentage break calculated point, as defined using drop percentage.

videos.label: Standard Results - Area @ Occurrence
videos.description: Area +/- Area under a curve defined by either a positive or negative about zero and by occurrence.

videos.label: Standard Results - Value @ Operation
videos.description: Additional Result Data Point - First, Middle Last, Delta, Max, Min An individual operation is interrogated to report the maximum, minimum, first, middle or last data point or the delta of start to finish of the operation.

videos.label: Standard Results - Area @ Operation
videos.description: Returns the area under the graph for the selected operation.

videos.label: Standard Results - Average @ Operation
videos.description: Returns the average value of the y axis for the operation.

videos.label: Standard Results - Value @ Digital Input
videos.description: Digital Line Additional Result Data Point - First, Middle Last, Delta, Max, Min Returns a value when a digital input changes state, reported result can be time, load, displacement, stress or strain. Also, the maximum, minimum, first, middle or last data point or the delta of start to finish of the operation.
